comment_text.ud2.8:1.3: Mūḷha here has the sense of “gone astray” in reference to obstructed labor that prevents birth; see MN 86:14.2.
comment_text.ud2.8:15.4: Lohitakumbhiya is probably a play on the common phrase lohakumbhiya, “copper pot”, which is commonly used in descriptions of hell. The boy, it seems, had an unusually rapid linguistic development.

root_text.ud2.8:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kuṇḍikāyaṁ viharati kuṇḍadhānavane.
root_text.ud2.8:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena suppavāsā koliyadhītā satta vassāni gabbhaṁ dhāreti.
root_text.ud2.8:1.4: Sattāhaṁ mūḷhagabbhā sā dukkhāhi tibbāhi kharāhi kaṭukāhi vedanāhi phuṭṭhā tīhi vitakkehi adhivāseti:
root_text.ud2.8:1.5: “sammāsambuddho vata so bhagavā yo imassa evarūpassa dukkhassa pahānāya dhammaṁ deseti;
root_text.ud2.8:1.6: suppaṭipanno vata tassa bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho yo imassa evarūpassa dukkhassa pahānāya paṭipanno;
root_text.ud2.8:1.7: susukhaṁ vata taṁ nibbānaṁ yatthidaṁ evarūpaṁ dukkhaṁ na saṁvijjatī”ti.
root_text.ud2.8:2.1: Atha kho suppavāsā koliyadhītā sāmikaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.ud2.8:2.2: “ehi tvaṁ, ayyaputta, yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkama; upasaṅkamitvā mama vacanena bhagavato pāde sirasā vandāhi; appābādhaṁ appātaṅkaṁ lahuṭṭhānaṁ balaṁ phāsuvihāraṁ puccha:
root_text.ud2.8:2.3: ‘suppavāsā, bhante, koliyadhītā bhagavato pāde sirasā vandati; appābādhaṁ appātaṅkaṁ lahuṭṭhānaṁ balaṁ phāsuvihāraṁ pucchatī’ti.
root_text.ud2.8:2.5: ‘suppavāsā, bhante, koliyadhītā satta vassāni gabbhaṁ dhāreti.
root_text.ud2.8:2.6: Sattāhaṁ mūḷhagabbhā sā dukkhāhi tibbāhi kharāhi kaṭukāhi vedanāhi phuṭṭhā tīhi vitakkehi adhivāseti—
root_text.ud2.8:2.7: sammāsambuddho vata so bhagavā yo imassa evarūpassa dukkhassa pahānāya dhammaṁ deseti;
root_text.ud2.8:2.8: suppaṭipanno vata tassa bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho yo imassa evarūpassa dukkhassa pahānāya paṭipanno;
root_text.ud2.8:2.9: susukhaṁ vata taṁ nibbānaṁ yatthidaṁ evarūpaṁ dukkhaṁ na saṁvijjatī’”ti.
root_text.ud2.8:3.1: “Paraman”ti kho so koliyaputto suppavāsāya koliyadhītāya paṭissutvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi.
root_text.ud2.8:3.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho koliyaputto bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud2.8:3.3: “suppavāsā, bhante, koliyadhītā bhagavato pāde sirasā vandati, appābādhaṁ appātaṅkaṁ lahuṭṭhānaṁ balaṁ phāsuvihāraṁ pucchati;
root_text.ud2.8:3.5: ‘suppavāsā, bhante, koliyadhītā satta vassāni gabbhaṁ dhāreti.
root_text.ud2.8:3.6: Sattāhaṁ mūḷhagabbhā sā dukkhāhi tibbāhi kharāhi kaṭukāhi vedanāhi phuṭṭhā tīhi vitakkehi adhivāseti—
root_text.ud2.8:3.7: sammāsambuddho vata so bhagavā yo imassa evarūpassa dukkhassa pahānāya dhammaṁ deseti;
root_text.ud2.8:3.8: suppaṭipanno vata tassa bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho yo imassa evarūpassa dukkhassa pahānāya paṭipanno;
root_text.ud2.8:3.9: susukhaṁ vata nibbānaṁ yatthidaṁ evarūpaṁ dukkhaṁ na saṁvijjatī’”ti.
root_text.ud2.8:4.1: “Sukhinī hotu suppavāsā koliyadhītā; arogā arogaṁ puttaṁ vijāyatū”ti.
root_text.ud2.8:4.2: Saha vacanā ca pana bhagavato suppavāsā koliyadhītā sukhinī arogā arogaṁ puttaṁ vijāyi.
root_text.ud2.8:5.1: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho so koliyaputto bhagavato bhāsitaṁ abhinanditvā anumoditvā uṭṭhāyāsanā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā yena sakaṁ gharaṁ tena paccāyāsi.
root_text.ud2.8:5.2: Addasā kho so koliyaputto suppavāsaṁ koliyadhītaraṁ sukhiniṁ arogaṁ arogaṁ puttaṁ vijātaṁ.
root_text.ud2.8:5.4: “acchariyaṁ vata bho, abbhutaṁ vata bho, tathāgatassa mahiddhikatā mahānubhāvatā, yatra hi nāmāyaṁ suppavāsā koliyadhītā saha vacanā ca pana bhagavato sukhinī arogā arogaṁ puttaṁ vijāyissatī”ti.
root_text.ud2.8:6.1: Atha kho suppavāsā koliyadhītā sāmikaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.ud2.8:6.2: “ehi tvaṁ, ayyaputta, yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkama; upasaṅkamitvā mama vacanena bhagavato pāde sirasā vandāhi:
root_text.ud2.8:6.3: ‘suppavāsā, bhante, koliyadhītā bhagavato pāde sirasā vandatī’ti;
root_text.ud2.8:6.5: ‘suppavāsā, bhante, koliyadhītā satta vassāni gabbhaṁ dhāreti.
root_text.ud2.8:6.6: Sattāhaṁ mūḷhagabbhā sā etarahi sukhinī arogā arogaṁ puttaṁ vijātā.
root_text.ud2.8:6.7: Sā sattāhaṁ buddhappamukhaṁ bhikkhusaṅghaṁ bhattena nimanteti.
root_text.ud2.8:6.8: Adhivāsetu kira, bhante, bhagavā suppavāsāya koliyadhītāya satta bhattāni saddhiṁ bhikkhusaṅghenā’”ti.
root_text.ud2.8:7.1: “Paraman”ti kho so koliyaputto suppavāsāya koliyadhītāya paṭissutvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi.
root_text.ud2.8:7.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so koliyaputto bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud2.8:8.1: “Suppavāsā, bhante, koliyadhītā bhagavato pāde sirasā vandati;
root_text.ud2.8:8.3: ‘suppavāsā, bhante, koliyadhītā satta vassāni gabbhaṁ dhāreti.
root_text.ud2.8:8.4: Sattāhaṁ mūḷhagabbhā sā etarahi sukhinī arogā arogaṁ puttaṁ vijātā.
root_text.ud2.8:8.5: Sā sattāhaṁ buddhappamukhaṁ bhikkhusaṅghaṁ bhattena nimanteti.
root_text.ud2.8:8.6: Adhivāsetu kira, bhante, bhagavā suppavāsāya koliyadhītāya satta bhattāni saddhiṁ bhikkhusaṅghenā’”ti.
root_text.ud2.8:9.1: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarena upāsakena buddhappamukho bhikkhusaṅgho svātanāya bhattena nimantito hoti.
root_text.ud2.8:9.2: So ca upāsako āyasmato mahāmoggallānassa upaṭṭhāko hoti.
root_text.ud2.8:9.3: Atha kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.ud2.8:9.4: “ehi tvaṁ, moggallāna, yena so upāsako tenupasaṅkama; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ upāsakaṁ evaṁ vadehi:
root_text.ud2.8:9.5: ‘suppavāsā, āvuso, koliyadhītā satta vassāni gabbhaṁ dhāresi.
root_text.ud2.8:9.6: Sattāhaṁ mūḷhagabbhā sā etarahi sukhinī arogā arogaṁ puttaṁ vijātā.
root_text.ud2.8:9.7: Sā sattāhaṁ buddhappamukhaṁ bhikkhusaṅghaṁ bhattena nimanteti.
root_text.ud2.8:9.8: Karotu suppavāsā koliyadhītā satta bhattāni, pacchā tvaṁ karissasī’ti.
root_text.ud2.8:10.1: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho āyasmā mahāmoggallāno bhagavato paṭissutvā yena so upāsako tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ upāsakaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud2.8:10.2: “suppavāsā, āvuso, koliyadhītā satta vassāni gabbhaṁ dhāreti.
root_text.ud2.8:10.3: Sattāhaṁ mūḷhagabbhā sā etarahi sukhinī arogā arogaṁ puttaṁ vijātā.
root_text.ud2.8:10.4: Sā sattāhaṁ buddhappamukhaṁ bhikkhusaṅghaṁ bhattena nimanteti.
root_text.ud2.8:10.5: Karotu suppavāsā koliyadhītā satta bhattāni, pacchā tvaṁ karissasī”ti.
root_text.ud2.8:11.1: “Sace me, bhante, ayyo mahāmoggallāno tiṇṇaṁ dhammānaṁ pāṭibhogo—
root_text.ud2.8:11.3: karotu suppavāsā koliyadhītā satta bhattāni, pacchāhaṁ karissāmī”ti.
root_text.ud2.8:11.4: “Dvinnaṁ kho te ahaṁ, āvuso, dhammānaṁ pāṭibhogo—
root_text.ud2.8:12.1: “Sace me, bhante, ayyo mahāmoggallāno dvinnaṁ dhammānaṁ pāṭibhogo—
root_text.ud2.8:12.3: karotu suppavāsā koliyadhītā satta bhattāni, pacchāhaṁ karissāmī”ti.
root_text.ud2.8:13.1: Atha kho āyasmā mahāmoggallāno taṁ upāsakaṁ saññāpetvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud2.8:13.3: karotu suppavāsā koliyadhītā satta bhattāni, pacchā so karissatī”ti.
root_text.ud2.8:14.1: Atha kho suppavāsā koliyadhītā sattāhaṁ buddhappamukhaṁ bhikkhusaṅghaṁ paṇītena khādanīyena bhojanīyena sahatthā santappesi sampavāresi,
root_text.ud2.8:14.2: tañca dārakaṁ bhagavantaṁ vandāpesi sabbañca bhikkhusaṅghaṁ.
root_text.ud2.8:15.1: Atha kho āyasmā sāriputto taṁ dārakaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud2.8:15.2: “kacci te, dāraka, khamanīyaṁ, kacci yāpanīyaṁ, kacci na kiñci dukkhan”ti?
root_text.ud2.8:15.3: “Kuto me, bhante sāriputta, khamanīyaṁ, kuto yāpanīyaṁ.
root_text.ud2.8:16.2: “putto me dhammasenāpatinā saddhiṁ mantetī”ti attamanā pamuditā pītisomanassajātā ahosi.
root_text.ud2.8:16.3: Atha kho bhagavā suppavāsaṁ koliyadhītaraṁ attamanaṁ pamuditaṁ pītisomanassajātaṁ viditvā suppavāsaṁ koliyadhītaraṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud2.8:17.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud2.8:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Kuṇḍiyā in the Kuṇḍadhāna Grove.
translation_text.ud2.8:1.3: Now at that time Suppavāsā the Koliyan had been pregnant for seven years, and in obstructed labor for seven days.
translation_text.ud2.8:1.4: While suffering painful, sharp, severe, acute feelings, three thoughts helped her endure:
translation_text.ud2.8:1.5: “Oh! The Blessed One is indeed a fully awakened Buddha, who teaches the Dhamma for giving up suffering such as this.
translation_text.ud2.8:1.6: Oh! The Saṅgha of the Buddha’s disciples is indeed practicing well, who practice for giving up suffering such as this.
translation_text.ud2.8:1.7: Oh! Extinguishment is so very blissful, where such suffering as this is not found.”
translation_text.ud2.8:2.2: “Please, master, go to the Buddha, and in my name bow with your head to his feet. Ask him if he is healthy and well, nimble, strong, and living comfortably.
translation_text.ud2.8:2.5: ‘Suppavāsā the Koliyan has been pregnant for seven years, and in obstructed labor for seven days.
translation_text.ud2.8:2.6: While suffering painful, sharp, severe, acute feelings, three thoughts help her endure:
translation_text.ud2.8:2.7: “Oh! The Blessed One is indeed a fully awakened Buddha, who teaches the Dhamma for giving up suffering such as this.
translation_text.ud2.8:2.8: Oh! The Saṅgha of the Buddha’s disciples is indeed practicing well, who practice for giving up suffering such as this.
translation_text.ud2.8:2.9: Oh! Extinguishment is so very blissful, where such suffering as this is not found.”’”
translation_text.ud2.8:3.1: “Excellent idea,” he replied. He went to the Buddha and told him of his wife’s struggles. The Buddha said:
translation_text.ud2.8:4.1: “May Suppavāsā the Koliyan be happy and healthy! May she give birth to a healthy child!”
translation_text.ud2.8:4.2: As soon as he spoke, Suppavāsā, happy and healthy, gave birth to a healthy child.
translation_text.ud2.8:5.1: Saying “Yes, sir,” the Koliyan gentleman approved and agreed with what the Buddha said. He got up from his seat, bowed, and respectfully circled the Buddha, keeping him on his right. Then he returned to his own house.
translation_text.ud2.8:5.2: He saw that his wife, happy and healthy, had given birth to a healthy child,
translation_text.ud2.8:5.4: “Oh lord, how incredible, how amazing! The Realized One has such psychic power and might! For as soon as he spoke, Suppavāsā, happy and healthy, gave birth to a healthy child.”
translation_text.ud2.8:5.5: He became uplifted and overjoyed, full of rapture and happiness.
translation_text.ud2.8:6.2: “Please, master, go to the Buddha, and in my name bow with your head to his feet.
translation_text.ud2.8:6.5: ‘Suppavāsā the Koliyan, who was pregnant for seven years, and in obstructed labor for seven days,
translation_text.ud2.8:6.6: is now happy and healthy and has given birth to a healthy child.
translation_text.ud2.8:6.7: She invites the mendicant Saṅgha headed by the Buddha to a meal for seven days.
translation_text.ud2.8:6.8: Sir, would the Buddha please accept seven meals from Suppavāsā?’”
translation_text.ud2.8:7.1: “Excellent idea,” he replied. He went to the Buddha, told him the good news, and conveyed his wife’s invitation.
translation_text.ud2.8:9.1: Now at that time a certain lay follower had already invited the Sangha of monks headed by the Buddha for the meal on the following day.
translation_text.ud2.8:9.2: That lay follower was Venerable Mahāmoggallāna’s supporter.
translation_text.ud2.8:9.3: Then the Buddha addressed Venerable Mahāmoggallāna,
translation_text.ud2.8:9.4: “Please, Moggallāna, go to that lay follower and say to him,
translation_text.ud2.8:9.5: ‘Suppavāsā the Koliyan, who was pregnant for seven years, and in obstructed labor for seven days,
translation_text.ud2.8:9.6: is now happy and healthy and has given birth to a healthy child.
translation_text.ud2.8:9.7: She invites the mendicant Saṅgha headed by the Buddha to a meal for seven days.
translation_text.ud2.8:9.8: Let Suppavāsā make seven meals, afterwards you can make yours.’
translation_text.ud2.8:10.1: “Yes, sir,” replied Mahāmoggallāna. He went to that lay follower and conveyed the Buddha’s request.
translation_text.ud2.8:11.1: “If, sir, Master Mahāmoggallāna can guarantee me three things—
translation_text.ud2.8:11.3: then let Suppavāsā make seven meals, afterwards I shall make mine.”
translation_text.ud2.8:11.6: But as for faith, you alone are the guarantor.”
translation_text.ud2.8:12.1: “If, sir Master Mahāmoggallāna can guarantee me two things—
translation_text.ud2.8:12.3: then let Suppavāsā make seven meals, afterwards I shall make mine.”
translation_text.ud2.8:13.1: Having persuaded that lay follower, Mahāmoggallāna went to the Buddha and said,
translation_text.ud2.8:13.3: Let Suppavāsā make seven meals, afterwards he shall make his.”
translation_text.ud2.8:14.1: For seven days Suppavāsā served and satisfied the Buddha with her own hands with delicious fresh and cooked foods.
translation_text.ud2.8:14.2: And she made her little boy bow to the Buddha and the mendicant Sangha.
translation_text.ud2.8:15.1: Then Sāriputta said to the boy,
translation_text.ud2.8:15.2: “I hope you’re keeping well, little boy; I hope you’re all right. I hope that you are not in pain.”
translation_text.ud2.8:15.3: “How could I be keeping well? How could I be getting by?
translation_text.ud2.8:15.4: For seven years I lived in a pot of blood.”
translation_text.ud2.8:16.2: “My child is conversing with the General of the Dhamma!” was uplifted and overjoyed, full of rapture and happiness.
translation_text.ud2.8:16.3: Knowing this, the Buddha said to her,
translation_text.ud2.8:16.4: “Would you like to have another child like this?”
translation_text.ud2.8:16.5: “Sir, I would like to have seven more children like this!”
translation_text.ud2.8:17.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud2.8:18.2: the disliked in the guise of the liked,
translation_text.ud2.8:18.3: suffering in the guise of happiness,

variant_text.ud2.8:1.2: kuṇḍikāyaṁ → kuṇḍiyāyaṁ (bj, sya-all, pts-vp-pli1) | kuṇḍadhānavane → kuṇḍiṭṭhānavane (sya-all, pts-vp-pli1)
variant_text.ud2.8:5.4: saha vacanā ca pana → saha vacanā pana (pts-vp-pli1); saha vacanā (?)
variant_text.ud2.8:9.8: pacchā tvaṁ karissasī’ti → pacchā so karissati (sya-all, pts-vp-pli1); karissasīti saññāpehi (mr)
variant_text.ud2.8:11.4: Dvinnaṁ kho te ahaṁ → dvinnaṁ kho tesaṁ (pts-vp-pli1); dvinnaṁ kho nesaṁ (mr)

reference_text.ud2.8:9.1: cck25.82, csp1ed18.96, csp2ed18.96, dr25.128, ms18Ud_124, pts-vp-pli17, sya25.97
